items.title: Drawing Connections with CSS Anchor Positioning
items.description: A practical exploration of CSS Anchor Positioning and the anchor() function to visually connect related elements without extra markup.
items.titleOriginal: Drawing Connections with CSS Anchor Positioning - Roland Franke

items.title: Honoring Mobile OS Text Size
items.description: If your users scale the text size in Android or iDeviceOS, that doesn’t always affect the size of text on a web page. It’s a function of browser and authored code, as opposed to a standardized approach. That may be changing. Support The current state of affairs in the three…

items.title: Revert new mobile floating and rounded UI
items.description: Here is some CSS for those like me who dislike the new mobile floating UI and the very aggressive radius/rounding. The header and navbar are now pinned to the top and bottom, and stretch across the screen. Unfortunately, Obsidian draws content underneath the Android status bar (I personally dislike when apps do this) so I had to use a safe area calculation for the top margin on the header.  For rounding, I tried targeting just specific elements but it was taking too much time in the dev console ...
items.titleOriginal: Revert new mobile floating and rounded UI

items.title: lashman Apps
items.description: Apps by lashman. Focused, minimal, privacy-first software.
items.content:
- Core Cooldown - A mindful break timer for Windows
- Whisper Voice - Local speech recognition for Windows
- TypoGenie - Turn Markdown into professional Word docs
- TutorialVault - Desktop video tutorial library manager
- OpenPylon - A local-first Kanban board for Windows
- Vesper - A distraction-free markdown reader for Windows
- ZeroClock - Local-first time tracker for freelancers
- Driftwood - Friendly AppImage manager for Linux desktops
- Outlay - Personal finance and expense tracker for Linux
- PixStrip - Batch image processor for Linux
- Nomina - Modern bulk file renamer for Windows
- EchoBoard - Self-hosted feedback platform
- Cinch - Trim and compress videos to a target size

items.title: An in-depth guide to customising lists with CSS
items.description: Richard Rutter helps you to make sense of list-style, list-item, ::marker, counters(), counter(), @counter-style, symbolic, symbols(), symbols and more to push your HTML and CSS lists to the next level.
items.titleOriginal: An in-depth guide to customising lists with CSS

items.title: Using the Browser’s canvas for Data Compression
items.description: Old browsers don’t have JavaScript compression APIs, so we store data in pixel color channels and export to PNG to compress data.
items.titleOriginal: Using the Browser’s <canvas> for Data Compression

items.title: NVM vs Mise - Choosing the Right Node
items.description: Compare NVM and Mise for Node.js version management. Learn key differences in performance, multi-language support, and auto-switching to choose the right tool.
items.titleOriginal: NVM vs Mise: Choosing the Right Node.js Version Manager | Better Stack Community

url: https://oneuptime.com/blog/post/2026-01-25-mise-tool-version-management/view#:~:text=mise%20is%20a%20polyglot%20version,tools%20with%20one%20consistent%20interface
title: "How to Use mise for Tool Version Management"
description: "Learn how to use mise (formerly rtx) to manage multiple runtime versions across projects, replacing tools like nvm, pyenv, and rbenv with a single, fast solution."

items.title: Sprites on the Web
items.description: In game development, it’s common to use spritesheets for animation, but this technique isn’t as widely used on the web. Which is a shame, because we can do some pretty cool stuff with sprites! In this post, we’ll share the niche CSS function you can use to leverage this technique, and explore some of the potential use cases.
items.titleOriginal: Sprites on the Web • Josh W. Comeau

items.title: Dopplr colours
items.description: Last year I was introduced to the idea of “Dopplr colours” in the IndieWeb community. This refers to an accent colour assigned to cities on the now-defunct travel website Dopplr.
